recalls.Description: Petzl America has announced a recall of its Nomic and Ergonomic ice climbing axes due to a potential fall hazard. The recalled Nomic axes (model U021AA00) and Ergonomic axes (model U022AA00) feature aluminum shafts, steel picks, and reinforced plastic grips. The Nomic axes are black, while the Ergonomic axes are orange. Serial numbers for both models can be found on a label located on the upper section of the shaft.
recalls.Title: Petzl America Issues Recall for Nomic and Ergonomic Ice Climbing Axes Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Petzl America at 877-807-3805, available from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email Returns@petzl.com or visit https://qr.petzl.com/a/recall-nomic and select 'Safety Recall' for further details.

Products.Name: Petzl Nomic Ice Climbing Axes
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 4,200 (In addition, about 1,160 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Nomic Ice Climbing Axe
Recalled Ergonomic Ice Climbing Axe
Location of serial number
Recalled Nomic and Ergonomic ice climbing axes

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 26 reports of the ice axe shafts breaking at the handle. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at REI, Moosejaw Mountaineering, and various specialty retailers across the country, as well as online at petzl.com, REI.com, and Backcountry.com from December 2017 to March 2022, priced between $380 and $440.

Importers.Name: Petzl America, Inc., of Salt Lake City, Utah

Hazards.Name: Potential for ice axe shaft to break at the handle during use, leading to a fall hazard and risk of serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled axes immediately and reach out to Petzl America for a complimentary repair kit. Registration is required at https://www.petzl.com/US/en/Sport/Contact?request=product-recall to obtain a pre-paid shipping label for returning the shaft. Petzl America will provide a repair kit that includes a new shaft, grip rest hardware, and assembly instructions.

recalls.metaDescription: Petzl recalls Nomic and Ergonomic ice climbing axes due to fall hazards. Stop using immediately and contact Petzl for a repair kit.

recalls.Description: C.A.M.P. has issued a recall for Nimbus Lock Carabiners available in Gun Metal/Green, Gun Metal/Fuchsia, and Gun Metal/Blue. These carabiners were sold individually or in packs, including the Nimbus Lock 3 Pack, Locker Draw KS, Energy CR3 Pack, and Energy Nova Pack. The recall specifically pertains to the carabiners themselves, identified by batch numbers 10 23, 12 23, 02 24, 09 24, 10 24, 12 24, 01 25, 02 25, and 06 25, which can be found on the side of the carabiner's bottom.
recalls.Title: C.A.M.P. Recalls Nimbus Lock Carabiners Due to Fall Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact C.A.M.P. at 877-421-2267 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email warranties@camp-usa.com. More information is available at www.camp-usa.com/nimbus-lock-safety-recall-information or by visiting www.camp-usa.com and selecting 'Safety Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Nimbus Lock Carabiners
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 12,600 (In addition, about 2,100 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Nimbus Lock Carabiners
Recalled Nimbus Lock Carabiner with location of batch number
Recalled Nimbus Lock Carabiners in the Nimbus Lock 3 Pack and the Locker Draw KS
Recalled Nimbus Lock Carabiners in the Energy CR3 Pack and the Energy Nova Pack

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 21 reports of the closing mechanism breaking. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: C.A.M.P. has documented 21 instances of the closing mechanism failing, but no injuries have been reported.

Importers.Name: C.A.M.P. USA Inc., of Golden, Colorado

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to fall from carabiner's faulty closing mechanism.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled carabiners and contact C.A.M.P. They will need to complete a recall form at www.camp-usa.com/nimbus-lock-safety-recall-registration-form, print a prepaid shipping label, and return the product. C.A.M.P. will inspect the returned items and send them back if they meet safety standards. Units failing inspection will be replaced at no cost.

recalls.metaDescription: C.A.M.P. recalls Nimbus Lock Carabiners due to a potential fall hazard. Immediate action is advised for consumers. Learn more here.

recalls.Description: Mammut Sports Group is recalling its Skywalker Pro Via Ferrata Sets, which are designed for mountain and rock climbing. These sets feature a blue cord and an orange and gray carabiner. The recall specifically affects model numbers 2040-02840, 2040-02850, and 2040-02870, which can be located on a tag inside the pouch of the product.
recalls.Title: Mammut Sports Group Recalls Skywalker Pro Via Ferrata Sets Due to Fall Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Mammut Sports Group at 802-800-2593 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email warranty.usa@mammut.com or visit https://www.mammut.com/us/en and click on the "Recall for Skywalker Pro Via Ferrata" banner, or access https://mammut.dani-o.com/voluntary-recall-mammut-skywalker-pro-via-ferrata-set for further details.

Products.Name: Skywalker Pro Via Ferrata Sets
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 580 (In addition, about 42 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Skywalker Pro Via Ferrata
Recalled Skywalker Pro Turn Via Ferrata
Recalled Skywalker Pro Via Ferrata Package
Style Number Location

Retailers.Name: Available at Jackson Hole Mountain Resort in Jackson Hole, Wyoming, Campsaver in Millville, Utah, and other outdoor retailers across the country from February 2023 through March 2025, priced between $150 and $280.

Importers.Name: Mammut Sports Group Inc., of Williston, Vermont

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to fall hazard from carabiner malfunction.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the Via Ferrata Sets immediately and contact Mammut Sports Group for a full refund. Return labels will be provided for the return of recalled products.

recalls.metaDescription: Mammut recalls Skywalker Pro Via Ferrata Sets due to fall hazards. Contact for refunds and return details.

recalls.Description: Black Diamond Equipment is recalling all BD Vision Climbing Harnesses due to a significant fall hazard that could lead to severe injuries or fatalities. This lightweight harness, suitable for technical alpine and ice climbing, is available in all sizes and features fixed leg loops. The harness is identifiable by the 'Black Diamond' logo on one leg loop and 'Vision' on the other, and it comes in black and white colors.
recalls.Title: Black Diamond Equipment Issues Recall for BD Vision Climbing Harnesses Due to Serious Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Black Diamond at 866-306-0865 from 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, or via email at VisionHarnessRecall@bdel.com. More details can be found online at https://www.blackdiamondequipment.com/en_US/content/product-recalls/ or by visiting www.blackdiamondequipment.com and navigating to 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: BD Vision Climbing Harness
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,580 (In addition, about 130 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Black Diamond Vision Harness
Recalled Black Diamond Vision Harness (label on side of leg loop)

Injuries.Name: Black Diamond has received one report involving the harness waist belt failing. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Liberty Mountain, Recreational Equipment Inc. (REI), various specialty outdoor retailers nationwide, and online at www.blackdiamondequipment.com, www.backcountry.com, and www.evo.com from January 2018 through February 2025, priced around $175.

Importers.Name: Black Diamond Equipment Ltd., of Salt Lake City, Utah

Hazards.Name: Potential degradation of the harness leading to failure and fall risk, which may cause serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease using the BD Vision harnesses immediately and contact Black Diamond for a full refund via credit card reimbursement or a one-time $200 credit for future purchases. Black Diamond will provide a pre-paid shipping label for returning the harness. They are also reaching out to all known buyers.

recalls.metaDescription: Black Diamond recalls BD Vision Climbing Harnesses due to fall hazards. Immediate action recommended for safety.

recalls.Description: Head Rush Technologies is recalling its TRUBLUE iQ and TRUBLUE iQ+ Auto Belay Devices due to a potential fall hazard. The TRUBLUE iQ model features a gray body with blue handles, while the TRUBLUE iQ+ includes an additional catch-and-hold brake. The LT versions have blue tags above the hand grip, and the XL versions are marked with a purple tag. Affected devices have specific serial number ranges that can be found on the handle and bottom of the unit.
recalls.Title: Head Rush Technologies Issues Recall for TRUBLUE iQ and TRUBLUE iQ+ Auto Belays Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Head Rush Technologies at 720-565-6885 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, email info@headrushtech.com, or visit https://info.headrushtech.com/hubfs/Product_Recalls/SA-0005-v01-en.pdf. More details are available at www.headrushtech.com by clicking on the 'Recall' banner.

Images.Caption: Recalled TruBlue iQ Auto Belay System
Recalled TruBlue iQ+ Auto Belay System
Serial Number Location
Serial Number Location

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 109 reports of incidents of slow retraction, including one injury involving the auto belay not retracting, resulting in a fractured leg.

Manufacturers.Name: Head Rush Technologies, of Louisville, Colorado

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online at www.headrushtech.com from May 2023 through December 2024, priced between $2,800 and $4,600.

Hazards.Name: Fall hazard due to potential failure to retract

recalls.metaDescription: Head Rush Technologies recalls TRUBLUE iQ and iQ+ Auto Belays due to fall hazards. Contact for free inspection and repair.

recalls.Description: ARVA is recalling its Neo BT Pro Avalanche Transceivers due to a potential defect that may lead to loss of emergency communication. These transceivers are characterized by a black plastic casing and an LCD screen situated below a red mark button. The model name NEO BT PRO is printed on the left side, and affected units have serial numbers ranging from NBP00052-1027 to NBP00287-1127 and NB00300-1127 to NB11675-0329, found inside the battery compartment lid.
recalls.Title: ARVA Recalls NEO BT PRO Avalanche Transceivers Due to Emergency Communication Failure Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach ARVA at 844-636-2782 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://us.arva-equipment.com/content/1488-recall and select "USA" for more details.

Products.Name: ARVA Neo BT Pro Avalanche Transceivers

Images.Caption: Recalled ARVA NEO BT PRO Avalanche Transceiver
Recalled ARVA NEO BT PRO Avalanche Transceiver
Recalled ARVA NEO BT PRO Avalanche Transceiver

Retailers.Name: Available at winter outdoor sporting goods stores and online at arva-equipment.com, backcountry.com, and Evo.com from September 2022 through January 2025, priced around $430.

Importers.Name: Nic Impex ARVA Corp., of Orem, Utah

Hazards.Name: Defective fuse may drain battery power, risking loss of emergency communication, potentially leading to serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled transceiver and contact NIC IMPEX ARVA CORP. for a complimentary inspection and repair, with a shipping label provided for return.

recalls.metaDescription: ARVA recalls Neo BT Pro Avalanche Transceivers due to a defect that risks emergency communication failure, posing serious safety threats.

recalls.Description: Setsmart is recalling its LoGest Climbing Ropes, which are available in lengths of 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, and 50 feet. The ropes come in two variations: one features a silver 'D' ring and a carabiner, while the other includes a heavy-duty metal hook with a blue stripe. Both types display 'LoGest' prominently on the ends in white text against black plastic. Consumers should be aware that these ropes may weaken and break, creating a serious fall hazard.
recalls.Title: Setsmart Issues Recall for LoGest Climbing Ropes Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Setsmart via email at LoGestRecall@gmail.com or visit www.LoGestRopeRecall.com.

Products.Name: LoGest Climbing Rope with Carabiner
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 22,500 (In addition, about 350 were sold in Canada and about 25 were sold in Mexico)

Images.Caption: Recalled LoGest Climbing Rope with Carabiner
Recalled LoGest Climbing Rope with Heavy-Duty Metal Hook

Injuries.Name: LoGest has received 13 reports of the recalled ropes breaking, including one report of a fall that resulted in a severe spinal cord injury.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from March 2021 to November 2024, priced between $20 and $70.

Importers.Name: Setsmart LLC, of Brooklyn, New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of weakening and breaking, leading to potential falls.

Remedies.Name: Cease use immediately, uninstall, and register for a full refund at www.LoGestRopeRecall.com. A signed form for disposal will be provided, and consumers must follow the instructions and email a photo of the disposed rope to LoGestRecall@gmail.com to complete the refund process. Refunds will be processed via Amazon to the original payment method. Amazon is reaching out to all known customers.

recalls.metaDescription: Setsmart recalls LoGest Climbing Ropes due to fall hazards. Stop using immediately and register for a refund.

recalls.Description: Black Diamond Equipment has issued a recall for all PIEPS Pro IPS Avalanche Transceivers due to a potential failure in the battery compartment that may prevent the device from powering on. These transceivers are made of black plastic, feature an LCD screen, and measure approximately 5 inches in length and 3 inches in width. The model name appears in yellow and white lettering on the front, and the serial numbers for affected units range from 2326 to 2445, located inside the battery compartment door.
recalls.Title: Black Diamond Equipment Recalls PIEPS Pro IPS Avalanche Transceivers Due to Emergency Communication Failure Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled avalanche transceivers immediately and reach out to Black Diamond at 866-306-0865 from 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday. For assistance, email 2024ProIPSrecall@bdel.com or visit https://www.blackdiamondequipment.com/en_US/content/product-recalls/ or www.blackdiamondequipment.com and select 'Product Recalls' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: PIEPS Pro IPS Avalanche Transceiver
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 770 (In addition, about 70 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled PIEPS PRO IPS Avalanche Transceiver - front
Recalled PIEPS PRO IPS Avalanche Transceiver - back
Transceiver's serial number location

Injuries.Name: Black Diamond has received one report of the battery compartment not operating correctly. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Alpenglow Sports, Cripple Creek Backcountry, EVO Gear, Liberty Mountain, Mountain Chalet, Mammoth Mountaineering, and other specialty outdoor retailers across the country, as well as online at www.backcountry.com, www.polaris.com, and www.blackdiamondequipment.com from February 2024 through November 2024, priced between $530 and $620.

Importers.Name: Black Diamond Equipment Ltd., of Salt Lake City, Utah

Hazards.Name: Risk of power failure in the battery compartment, potentially preventing operation during emergencies.

Remedies.Name: Consumers can receive a full refund or a free repair, including shipping, without needing proof of purchase. A pre-paid shipping label will be provided for returning the transceivers.

recalls.metaDescription: Black Diamond recalls PIEPS Pro IPS Avalanche Transceivers due to potential power failure risk. Stop using immediately and contact for remedy.

recalls.Description: Petzl America has issued a recall for three models of climbing harnesses: the GYM harness (Model No. C032AA00), the PANDION harness (Model Nos. C029AA00 and C029AA01), and the PANJI harness (Model No. C028AA00). These harnesses are commonly used in climbing gyms, adventure parks, and by outdoor enthusiasts. Serial numbers can be found on a label inside each harness, and specific ranges are identified for the recalled models. Consumers should check their harnesses against the serial numbers provided to determine if they are affected by this recall.
recalls.Title: Petzl America Recalls GYM, PANDION, and PANJI Climbing Harnesses Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach Petzl America, Inc. toll-free at 877-807-3805 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, or email Returns@petzl.com. Additional information is available online at https://www.petzl.com/US/en/Operators/safety-alerts or www.petzl.com/US/en/Operators.

Products.Name: Petzl GYM Climbing Harness
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 22,000 (In addition, about 5,800 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Petzl GYM Climbing Harness (Model No. C032AA00)
Recalled Petzl PANDION Climbing Harness (Model Nos. C029AA00 and C029AA01)
Recalled Petzl PANJI Climbing Harness (Model No. C028AA00)
Format of serial numbers located on a label inside of the recalled harnesses

Injuries.Name: There has been one report of a user in the United States suffering serious injuries from a fall.

Retailers.Name: Available online and in-store at KarstSports, PeakTrading, Backcountry, OmniProGear, Moosejaw, and other outdoor retailers from January 2019 to May 2024, priced between $59.95 and $69.95.

Importers.Name: Petzl America, Inc., of Salt Lake City, Utah

Hazards.Name: Risk of falling due to improper harness usage

recalls.metaDescription: Petzl recalls GYM, PANDION, and PANJI climbing harnesses due to fall hazards. Check your harness serial number and contact Petzl for a free repair.

recalls.Description: Head Rush Technologies is recalling its TRUBLUE Speed Auto Belay Devices, available in 12.5m and 16m lengths. These devices are designed to ensure safe descents for climbers after speed climbing events. The devices feature a gray body with blue handles; the 12.5m model has a black webbing handgrip, while the 16m model has a blue webbing handgrip. Each device is labeled with the model TRUBLUE Speed TBSD150-20 and a serial number that falls within the range of TBSD00098 to TBSD01180.
recalls.Title: Head Rush Technologies Recalls TRUBLUE Speed Auto Belay Devices Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Head Rush Technologies at 720-565-6885 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, or email inf@headrushtech.com. More information is available online at https://info.headrushtech.com/hubfs/Product_Recalls/2024_TRUBLUESpeed_Limited_Recall.pdf or by visiting www.headrushtech.com and clicking on the 'Recall' banner.

Images.Caption: Recalled Head Rush Technologies TRUBLUE Speed Auto Belay Device (12.5 Meter Version)
Recalled Head Rush Technologies TRUBLUE Speed Auto Belay Device (16 Meter Version)
Location of Model Number and Serial Number

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one incident report involving brake failure, causing the user to fall, resulting in a broken ankle.

Manufacturers.Name: Head Rush Technologies, of Louisville, Colorado

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.headrushtech.com from May 2017 to July 2021, priced between $2,800 and $2,900.

Hazards.Name: Backup brake failure leading to fall risk and potential serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled devices and contact Head Rush Technologies for a complimentary inspection and repair. The company is reaching out to all known buyers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Head Rush Technologies recalls TRUBLUE Speed Auto Belay Devices due to fall hazards. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description:
Head Rush Technologies has announced a recall of TRUBLUE iQ Auto Belay Devices, designed to ensure safe descents for climbers. These devices feature a gray body with blue handles, while the TRUBLUE IQ+ includes a catch-and-hold brake. The LT models have blue tags above the hand grip, and the XL models are marked with a purple tag. The recall affects specific serial number ranges for various models, which are detailed below. 

The affected models include the TRUBLUE iQ LT, TRUBLUE iQ XL, TRUBLUE iQ+ LT, and TRUBLUE iQ+ XL. These devices may fail to retract properly, presenting a significant fall hazard that could lead to serious injury or even death. Consumers are urged to check their devices against the provided serial number ranges to determine if their unit is affected.
recalls.Title: Head Rush Technologies Issues Recall for TRUBLUE iQ Auto Belay Devices Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Head Rush Technologies at 720-565-6885 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, or via email at info@headrushtech.com. More information is available online at https://info.headrushtech.com/hubfs/Product_Recalls/2024_TRUBLUEiQ_Voluntary_Recall.pdf or by visiting www.headrushtech.com and clicking on the '2024 TruBlue iQ Voluntary Recall' banner.

Products.Name: TRUBLUE iQ Auto Belay Device

Manufacturers.Name: Head Rush Technologies, of Louisville, Colorado

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.headrushtech.com from November 2022 through February 2024, priced between $2,800 and $4,600.

Hazards.Name: Fall hazard due to potential failure to retract

Remedies.Name: Free inspection and repair for affected devices

recalls.metaDescription: Head Rush Technologies recalls TRUBLUE iQ Auto Belay Devices due to a fall hazard. Stop using these devices and contact for a free inspection.

recalls.Description: Black Diamond Equipment is recalling the Neve Strap Crampons and Accessory Kits, identified by Style No. 400073, with date codes 01-2021, 05-2022, 07-2023, 01-2024, and SS1271. These codes can be found on the rubber heel strap pull-tab and the underside of the toe piece beneath the anti-balling plate. The crampons, designed for ice climbing, feature aluminum plates with spikes that attach to boots, characterized by bright green metal plates and black plastic components with webbing straps for ankle security. This recall also includes the Neve Strap Binding Accessory Kit, Style No. 400683, and Neve Strap Heel Cup Screw & Nuts, Style No. 400687, sold separately as accessories.
recalls.Title: Black Diamond Equipment Recalls Neve Strap Crampons and Accessory Kits Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Black Diamond Equipment at 866-306-0865 from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, email NeveStrapRecall@bdel.com, or visit https://www.blackdiamondequipment.com/en_US/content/neve-strap-recall/ or www.blackdiamondequipment.com and click on 'Recalls' for further details.

Products.Name: Neve Strap Crampons and Accessory Kits
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 350 (In addition, about 28 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Neve Strap Crampon
Date code located on the underside of the toe piece of the recalled Neve Strap Crampon

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of incidents of the crampons becoming loose or the heel cup becoming detached by hikers. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Black Diamond stores, REI, and various retailers nationwide, as well as online at blackdiamond.com, backcountry.com, and REI.com from January 2024 through May 2024, priced around $180.

Importers.Name: Black Diamond Equipment, of Salt Lake City, Utah

Hazards.Name: Potential for heel cup detachment due to loosening screws, creating a fall risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled products and contact Black Diamond Equipment for a full refund, including a prepaid return label for product return.

recalls.metaDescription: Black Diamond recalls Neve Strap Crampons and Accessory Kits due to fall hazards. Contact for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description: Salewa USA has announced a recall for the orange Wild Country 'Ropeman 1' ascenders, which are commonly used in mountaineering and rock climbing. These products, manufactured in July 2022, are identifiable by the batch number 05A0722 printed in white on the side. The failure of the ascenders' teeth to engage can pose serious safety risks, making it challenging or impossible for users to ascend safely.
recalls.Title: Salewa USA Issues Recall for Wild Country Ropeman 1 Ascenders Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Wild Country at 844-412-7013 from 11 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at Ropeman1Recall@wildcountry.com. More information is available online at https://www.wildcountry.com/en-us/product-recalls or by visiting https://www.wildcountry.com/en-us/ and selecting 'Ropeman 1 Voluntary Recall' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Wild Country Ropeman 1 Ascenders
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 7,700 (In addition, about 297 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Wild Country Ropeman 1 Ascender
Recalled Wild Country Ropeman 1 Ascender

Retailers.Name: Available at REI and various specialty outdoor retailers across the country, as well as online at www.wildcountry.com, www.backcountry.com, and www.campsaver.com from December 2022 through April 2024 for approximately $55.

Importers.Name: Salewa USA LLC, of Boulder, Colorado

Hazards.Name: Teeth failure preventing engagement, complicating ascent

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled ascenders immediately and contact Wild Country for a return and free replacement. Alternatively, returns can be made directly to Wild Country or any authorized dealer for a free replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: Salewa USA recalls Wild Country Ropeman 1 ascenders due to safety concerns. Learn how to return and replace your product.
